title: Shiro Sagisu Evangelion jazz arrangments
wikitext:
[]: Shiro Sagisu Evangelion jazz arrangments             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 23, 2014 6:04 pm http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3353.html http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3360.html Also: http://www.evageeks.org/2014/10/shiro-sagisus-new-evangelion-jazz-album/ Tracklist: 01: Weekend, the introduction (by all star singers) 02: Welcome to the Tokyo III jazz club (by all star orchestra) 03: Swingin’ A1 (by all star bigband) 04: Come sweet death, second impact (by Megumi-H) 05: Barefoot in the club (by all star bigband) 06: Final decision at the milestone (by all star combo) 07: Emptiness, the longest (by all star orchestra) 08: Thanatos, cool again! (by all star bigband) 09: Dilemmatic triangle opera (by Megumi-H) 10: A8, mellow slow-jam (by all star combo) 11: Theme Q, suppa-duppa! (by all star bigband) 12: The Image of black me (by Megumi-H)

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Oct 23, 2014 7:18 pm 12: The Image of black me (by Megumi-H)

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Thu Oct 23, 2014 7:19 pm Well it's about time! I wanna hear me some Eva-Jazz! (I guess the Thanatos from EoE was Jazz anyway, but this might be different.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mbryo » Sun Oct 26, 2014 1:12 am Maybe it'll be similar to Eva Vox? Well they are all songs I never heard of. If Hayashibara Megumi's singing them, she better sing them in her usual voice, not the Rei voice.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 26, 2014 2:30 am Mbryo wrote: Maybe it'll be similar to Eva Vox? Well they are all songs I never heard of. If Hayashibara Megumi's singing them, she better sing them in her usual voice, not the Rei voice.

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:17 pm As a fan of both jazz and especially Shiro Sagisu's music, this is fantastic! Only downside is waiting the next two months for the album to come out. ECH. In the meantime, I'm gonna do me some investigation on the names for the songs and see what they hint towards in the full album. (This can be put up on the first post for the sake of convenience if I've done a good enough job) BIG PILE OF TEXT SO SPOILERED SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Oct 26, 2014 5:33 pm I already have a record of jazz covers of Eva tracks .

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Sun Oct 26, 2014 7:57 pm Very interested in this. Jazz and Evangelion are two things I enjoy immensely.

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Thu Dec 25, 2014 7:25 am Topic bump! The album's out now, with this as its cover art if anyone wants to change the original post: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/pictures/l/01/02/KICA-3219.jpg (Done by Moyoco Anno if anyone's curious as to why she's mentioned on the cover) So far, only three tracks have been posted onto YT: -Swinging A1 ( Rei I - Hilariously jazzy edition) -Emptiness, the Longest ( Flow of Emptiness - Jazz-Orchestral) -Theme Q, Suppa-Duppa! ( Theme Q from 3.0 - New jazz cover, as opposed to reusing the Super Sax version) It sounds fantastic so far and I'll have to try get a copy of it over the holidays. Heck, I might even put up a review on this website for y'all to check out.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sat Jan 10, 2015 2:17 pm The album is indeed available if you know where to look. I've given it a listen; for the most part, I'm not too impressed (Sagisu's past work, and the 2.0 and 3.0 OSTs in particular, are much better) and it can be hard to hear any connection at all. That said, track 3 "COME SWEET DEATH,SECOND IMPACT" (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aLifQYjYUH0) has been growing on me (give it at least 2 minutes to go somewhere), and I liked track 10, "THEME Q,SUPPA-DUPPA!" (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3q4oUleJhgA). But LMAO at the narrator. What the heck.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 10, 2015 3:14 pm Just checked out a couple of the tracks. Good stuff! I've been looking for more jazz influences for my own work, and coupled with my love for Eva this is amazing. Thanks for sharing!

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Jan 10, 2015 4:42 pm Can the 4th Rebuild movie just be this?

[]:             Quote Post by Caliburn » Thu Jan 22, 2015 12:21 am Aww yeah, more Evangelion jazz, this time from Shiro Sagisu himself! Nice!
summary: []: Shiro Sagisu Evangelion jazz arrangments             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 23, 2014 6:04 pm http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3353.html http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3360.html Also: http://www.evageeks.org/2014/10/shiro-sagisus-new-evangelion-jazz-album/ Tracklist: 01: Weekend, the introduction (by all star singers) 02: Welcome to the Tokyo III jazz club (by all star orchestra) 03: Swingin’ A1
